latest.title: New Strategic Partnerships
latest.content:
14 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2107E
Thruvision Group PLC
14 May 2026
Reach - non-regulatory announcement
14 May 2026
Thruvision Group plc
New Strategic Partnerships
Thruvision Group plc (AIM: THRU),
a leading provider of walk-through security technology ("Thruvision" or the "Company" and, together with its subsidiary undertakings, the "Group"),
today announces the addition of RedteQ and Bavak Security Group to its Value-Added Reseller (VAR) network on a non-exclusive basis.
The Company has entered into agreements to enable both VARs to provide product supply, installation and commissioning, support and maintenance, and end-user training services for Thruvision products in our Entrance and Customs markets.
Victoria Balchin, Thruvision CEO commented:
"We are delighted to have finalised these new strategic partnerships with RedteQ and Bavak, both of which share Thruvision's commitment to quality and customer service. Indirect sales through trusted partners are an increasingly important part of our business and are critical to our ability to scale rapidly and cost-effectively. We are partnering with RedteQ for their system integration expertise in the UK and their reputation within the UK government sector and with Bavak following recommendations from existing customers in the Netherlands. We look forward to working with both RedteQ and Bavak to increase our reach to key clients in the Government and Critical Infrastructure sectors."
RedteQ
RedteQ, headquartered in Farnborough, UK, will cover the United Kingdom.
Alan Redpath, Founder and Managing Director, commented:
"We are proud to announce our partnership with British manufacturer and supplier Thruvision. RedteQ has chosen Thruvision for its highly accurate visual threat detection systems. Thruvision's technology complements our leading checkpoint security solutions, further strengthening the high-threat detection capabilities we supply, install, maintain, and support nationally across the UK and internationally for our valued customer base."
Bavak Security Group
Bavak Security Group ("Bavak"), based in Noordwijk, Netherlands, will provide the services in Belgium, the Netherlands and Luxembourg.
Chris Reudink, CCO of Bavak commented:
"In environments where predictability and continuity are essential, our partnership with Thruvision delivers non-intrusive detection capabilities that enhance security without disrupting operations. This represents an important added capability in our ambition to be the one-stop shop for the full spectrum of physical security challenges faced by critical infrastructure."
For further information please contact:
Thruvision Group plc
+44 (0)1235 425 400
Tom Black, Executive Chairman
Victoria Balchin, Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer
Allenby Capital Ltd
(Nominated Adviser & Broker)
+44 (0)20 3328 5656
James Reeve (Corporate Finance)
Jos Pinnington / Amrit Nahal (Sales)
About Thruvision
Thruvision is a leading international developer, manufacturer and supplier of walk-through security technology. Its technology is deployed in more than 30 countries around the world by government and commercial organisations in a wide range of security situations, where large numbers of people need to be screened quickly, safely and efficiently. Thruvision's patented technology is capable of detecting concealed metallic and non-metallic objects in real time using an advanced AI-based detection algorithm. The Group has offices and manufacturing capabilities in the UK and US.
About RedteQ
RedteQ Analytical Ltd, based in Hampshire, UK, specialises in advanced security screening and detection technologies, alongside nationwide maintenance and support services. (www.redteq.com)
About Bavak
Bavak is an international security integrator specialising in the protection of critical infrastructure and other high-risk environments. The company designs, integrates and maintains advanced security solutions that combine physical, electronic and cyber security into one integrated security architecture. Operating internationally from its headquarters in the Netherlands, Bavak supports governments, defence organisations and operators of mission-critical infrastructure with end-to-end solutions including perimeter protection, access control, screening systems, command and control platforms and integrated security management systems. By combining engineering expertise, system integration and long-term operational support, Bavak delivers scalable, reliable and future-proof security solutions focused on continuity, resilience and operational performance in the most demanding environments. (www.bavak.com).
Important information
This announcement may include statements that are, or may be deemed to be, "forward-looking statements" (including words such as "believe", "expect", "estimate", "intend", "anticipate" and words of similar meaning). Save as required by applicable law, the Company undertakes no obligation to publicly revise any forward-looking statements in this announcement, whether following any change in its expectations or to reflect events or circumstances after the date of this announcement.
About Reach announcements

Information required to be notified under the AIM Rules for Companies, Market Abuse Regulation or other regulation would be disseminated as an RNS regulatory announcement and not on Reach.

Thruvision (AIM:THRU, "Thruvision" or the "Group"), a leading international provider of walk-through security technology, today announces an update to its blocklisting application.
As all outstanding options under the Thruvision Group plc Sharesave Scheme that were covered by the block listing application have now been exercised or have lapsed, no further shares will be allotted pursuant to this block listing.

latest.title: FY26 Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5269Z
Thruvision Group PLC
08 April 2026
8 April 2026
Thruvision Group plc
FY26 Trading Update
Thruvision Group plc (AIM: THRU),
a leading provider of walk-through security technology ("Thruvision" or the "Company" and, together with its subsidiary undertakings, the "Group"),
today announces a trading update for the year ended 31 March 2026 ("FY26").
Highlights:
·
FY26 revenue has increased by 45% to approximately £6.0 million (FY25: £4.2 million), in line with Board expectations previously communicated in November 2025 of revenue between £5 and £7 million.
·
Order backlog at 31 March 2026 was £1.3 million, with order intake for FY26 of £7.1 million. This backlog will be delivered mostly in H1 FY27.
·
The Group's cash balance on 31 March 2026 was £2.0 million (31 March 2025: £0.4 million), following a fundraise of £2.6 million, after costs, in July 2025.
FY26 revenue grew by £1.8 million or 45% to £6.0 million. This has been underpinned by strong performance in Asia where the award of two material orders totaling £2.7 million formed the bulk of the regional revenue. Our indirect sales strategy in Asia is based on a small number of strong regional partnerships, which we intend to strengthen further over time. The UK and Europe were weaker than FY25, despite strong performance in the Prisons market. Our core market in this region, Retail Distribution, declined due to broader economic conditions, coupled with
the ongoing need to rebuild our direct sales capability and regional partnerships.
The US grew modestly, due to strong performance from Aviation. Retail Distribution in the US was weaker than expected and we are addressing this with investment in our direct sales force which we expect to lead to a significant improvement in FY27.
Victoria Balchin, CEO, commented:
"It's very pleasing to see a return to revenue growth as the steps we have taken to improve both our direct sales force and our partnerships start to take effect. Whilst work remains to be done, our new 81 Series product has been enthusiastically received and the benefits of deploying Thruvision are increasingly appreciated across our markets. Our challenge is now one of investing in our sales and marketing capability and this is the central focus of the team as we start our new year."
For further information please contact:
Thruvision Group plc
+44 (0)1235 425 400
Tom Black, Executive Chairman
Victoria Balchin, Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer
Allenby Capital Ltd
(Nominated Adviser & Broker)

James Reeve
Jos Pinnington / Amrit Nahal (Sales)

Its technology is deployed in more than 30 countries around the world by government and commercial organisations in a wide range of security situations, where large numbers of people need to be screened quickly, safely and efficiently. Thruvision's patented technology is capable of detecting concealed metallic and non-metallic objects in real time using an advanced AI-based detection algorithm. The Group has offices and manufacturing capabilities in the UK and US.

latest.title: US Aviation Contract Award
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4861W
Thruvision Group PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Thruvision Group plc
Thruvision secures further U.S. aviation contract award
from Greater Orlando Aviation Authority
Thruvision Group plc (AIM: THRU),
a leading provider of walk-through security technology ("Thruvision" or the "Company" and, together with its subsidiary undertakings, the "Group"),
today announces that the Greater Orlando Aviation Authority ("GOAA") has awarded Thruvision a contract for five 81-Series systems to support aviation worker screening operations at Orlando International Airport, Florida.
Highlights:
·
The total contract value is US$0.6 million (£0.4 million at current exchange rates), with equipment delivery expected by 31 March 2026.
·
It is for five 81-Series systems with a battery-powered base for mobile deployment and includes two years of enhanced support. The battery-powered base is a new feature only available for 81-Series products.
·
This contract follows the recent award for aviation worker screening systems at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport announced in December 2025.
·
This brings the total number of US airports using Thruvision systems to five and provides further evidence of the growing adoption of Thruvision's non-contact screening technology in the airport sector.
·
Airports are facing increasing regulatory scrutiny and operational pressure at employee access points. Thruvision's mobile, high-throughput systems are designed to enhance security effectiveness while maintaining workforce flow and minimising operational disruption - a critical requirement for large aviation hubs.
Together, these recent U.S. awards underscore growing demand for aviation worker screening solutions that improve compliance, operational efficiency and the employee screening experience. Thruvision's technology is deployed globally across airports and critical infrastructure environments where safety, throughput and reliability are mission critical.
Kevin Gramer, Senior Vice President, Thruvision Americas, who recently participated as a panellist at the American Association of Airport Executives (AAAE) Security Summit in Washington DC, commented:
"This order reinforces our growing position in the U.S. aviation market. Airports need worker screening solutions that are mobile, compliant, and affordable-without compromising security or throughput. Thruvision systems are designed to give operators that flexibility, helping them strengthen employee screening while keeping operations moving efficiently."
For further information please contact:
Thruvision Group plc
+44 (0)1235 425 400
Tom Black, Executive Chairman
Victoria Balchin, Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer
Allenby Capital Ltd
(Nominated Adviser & Broker)
+44 (0)20 3328 5656
James Reeve / Piers Shimwell (Corporate Finance)
Jos Pinnington / Amrit Nahal (Sales)

Thruvision is a leading international developer, manufacturer and supplier of walk-through security technology. Its technology is deployed in more than 30 countries around the world by government and commercial organisations in a wide range of security situations, where large numbers of people need to be screened quickly, safely and efficiently. Thruvision's patented technology is capable of detecting concealed metallic and non-metallic objects in real time using an advanced AI-based detection algorithm. The Group has offices and manufacturing capabilities in the UK and US.

company_30s.what_they_do: Thruvision Group plc designs, builds and sells video‑surveillance hardware and AI‑driven analytics software for airports, retail chains, government sites and transport hubs.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global security market is moving toward smart, data‑centric solutions. Thruvision’s analytics give it an edge over traditional CCTV and its contracts in aviation and retail show it is tapping high‑value sectors.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently cleared a blocklisting status, won several new contracts and is reporting incremental revenue growth, but it remains in a loss‑making phase and trades at a tiny fraction of its pre‑crash valuation.

rns_narrative: The company has shifted from a period of regulatory uncertainty to one of active contract acquisition—new aviation, retail and custodial deals are being signed.
Management is also focusing on stabilising cash flow through FY26 updates and a strategic partnership programme.
Recent announcements indicate the company is moving from survival mode toward a modest growth trajectory.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism after regulatory relief
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few investors point to the aviation contracts and retail network as evidence that the market has under‑priced the firm.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Concerns remain about the company’s cash burn, past regulatory issues and whether it can sustain its current revenue mix.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from silence to tentative hope following the blocklisting cancellation and a wave of contract wins.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest spikes, so any news could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: A Q2 earnings release that beats analyst expectations.
Announcement of a major airline or retail partnership.
Regulatory clearance or a positive sign‑off on the blocklisting review.
A volume spike that exceeds three times the 20‑day average.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for volume surges, a move above the 50‑day moving average, a positive earnings surprise or a new contract award. Any of these would signal a mean‑reversion bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX compression score shows the stock is already in a tight trough; a bounce is likely once a catalyst confirms that the market has over‑reacted.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is trading at a deep trough, and a regulatory lift plus fresh contracts create a narrative that the market has undervalued it. Historical RED signals (deep crashes) have delivered ~160% average returns, so a bounce to pre‑crash levels could more than double the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm still burns cash and faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny. If it fails to secure new deals or a regulatory setback occurs, the price could stay near zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside >150% versus downside limited to the current price – a risk/reward ratio better than 3:1 if the bounce materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 50‑day moving average with a stop 5% below that level. Size the position so that each trade risks 1–2% of capital.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until volume spikes 3× the 20‑day average or a new contract is announced; then enter on a break of the short‑term trend.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified – the firm is not facing imminent delisting or bankruptcy, so the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Thruvision is a deep‑crashed, regulator‑cleared security‑tech player that just landed new contracts; if the market reacts, the upside could exceed 150% with risk capped at the current price.
